LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF LAUREE PRIETO'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S INTERROGATORIES; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 24, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 SO THAT THE PARTIES CAN FURTHER MEET AND CONFER ON THE DISCOVERY THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THIS MOTION. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER, AND MUST DO SO IN PERSON OR ON THE TELEPHONE, RATHER THAN BY THE EXCHANGE OF LETTERS OR EMAILS. BY NO LATER THAN JUNE 20, 2013, THE PARTIES ARE TO PROVIDE THE COURT A WRITTEN UPDATE, IDENTIFYING BY INTERROGATORY NUMBER THOSE INTERROGATORIES IN THIS MOTION, IF ANY, THAT ARE STILL IN DISPUTE BETWEEN THEM. THIS MAY BE PROVIDED TO THE COURT IN THE FORM OF A LETTER OR A SHORT BRIEF OF NO MORE THAN 3 PAGES. THERE WILL BE NO ADDITIONAL ARGUMENT IN THIS WRITTEN UPDATE. A COURTESY COPY OF THE WRITTEN UPDATE MUST BE DELIVERED TO THE COURT BY 4:00 P.M. ON JUNE 20, 2013, CLEARLY STATING THAT IT IS TO BE BROUGHT TO THE COURT'S ATTENTION FOR THE JUNE 24, 2013 CALENDAR. IF THE JUNE 24, 2013 HEARING DATE IS NOT MUTUALLY CONVENIENT TO THE PARTIES, THE PARTIES MUST MEET AND CONFER ON ANOTHER SUITABLE DATE AND BE PREPARED TO ADDRESS THIS AT THE HEARING ON JUNE 11, 2013. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT B&H EDUCATION, INC. DBA MARINELLO SCHOOLS OF BEAUTY'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $5325.00 AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. A NON-RESIDENT PARTY WITNESS MAY BE DEPOSED IN CALIFORNIA. (GLASS V. SUP.CT. (1988) 204 CAL.APP.3D 1048, 1052 - 1053.) THE FACTORS ENUMERATED IN CCP 2025.260 FAVOR COMPELLING PLAINTIFF TO BE DEPOSED IN CALIFORNIA. PLAINTIFF ELECTED TO SUE IN CALIFORNIA. ALL OF THE DOCUMENTS, OTHER WITNESSES AND ATTORNEYS ARE IN CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANT HAS OFFERED TO PAY FOR PLAINTIFF'S EXPENSES AND TO ACCOMMODATE PLAINTIFF'S SCHEDULE. THE DEPOSITION SHALL BE LIMITED TO TWO CONSECUTIVE DAYS. DEFENDANT SHALL PAY FOR PLAINTIFF'S REASONABLE EXPENSES AND COSTS TO TRAVEL TO CALIFORNIA FOR THE DEPOSITION. REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. IN ADDITION, THE COURT ORDERED THE PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE THE DEFENDANT WITH PROPOSED DEPOSITION DATES BY JUNE 7, 2013. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: PEPPINA HARLOW, CSR # 7433 (TEL.310.557.3400)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF LAUREE PRIETO'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS IS CONTINUED FROM MAY-15-2013 TO MAY-22-2013 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL A. RENNE; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH OR MODIFY DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS IS DENIED. THE SERVICE OF THE SUBPOENA IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFF'S REFUSAL TO PROVIDE DISCOVERY INFORMATION REGARDING URBS WAS NEITHER A VIOLATION OF ANY RULE OF PROFESSIONAL CONDUCT NOR AN INAPPROPRIATE EX PARTE COMMUNICATION. IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY REFUSAL, DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS REGARDING THE SUBPOENA WERE REASONABLE. REGARDING THE SCOPE OF THE DOCUMENTS TO BE PRODUCED, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MEET HER BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE THAT ANY OF THE COVERED MATERIALS SHOULD NOT BE PRODUCED. AMONG OTHER REASONS, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH CRC 3.1345 OR MADE A COMPARABLE SHOWING. PLAINTIFF'S POSITION REGARDING HER MOTION IS NOT SUBSTANTIALLY JUSTIFIED. ACCORDINGLY, PURSUANT TO CCP 1987.2(A) DEFENDANT IS AWARDED $5,850 IN SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HER COUNSEL, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: PEPPINA HARLOW, CSR # 7433 (TEL.310.557.3400)

THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER, AND MUST DO SO IN PERSON OR ON THE TELEPHONE, RATHER THAN BY THE EXCHANGE OF LETTERS OR EMAILS. BY NO LATER THAN JUNE 20, 2013, THE PARTIES ARE TO PROVIDE THE COURT A WRITTEN UPDATE, IDENTIFYING BY REQUEST NUMBER THOSE DOCUMENT REQUESTS IN THIS MOTION, IF ANY, THAT ARE STILL IN DISPUTE BETWEEN THEM. THIS MAY BE PROVIDED TO THE COURT IN THE FORM OF A LETTER OR A SHORT BRIEF NO MORE THAN 3 PAGES. THERE WILL BE NO ADDITIONAL ARGUMENT IN THIS WRITTEN UPDATE. A COURTESY COPY OF THE WRITTEN UPDATE MUST BE DELIVERED TO THE COURT BY 4:00 P.M. ON JUNE 20, 2013, CLEARLY STATING THAT IT IS TO BE BROUGHT TO THE COURT'S ATTENTION FOR THE JUNE 24, 2013 CALENDAR. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT B&H EDUCATION, INC. DBA MARINELLO SCHOOLS OF BEAUTY'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions. The Court has received the correspondence of both counsel dated June 7, 2013 indicating all of the areas of agreement with regard to discovery and the tentative ruling issued on May 29, 2013. The Court will adopt the areas of agreement as the ruling of the Court. As to the areas on which the parties did not reach agreement, the Court rules as follows: Requests for Production 2, 8, and 22: the Court reiterates its earlier tentative ruling that within 20 days, Plaintiff shall provide verified further responses and produce documents. If there are document requests where Plaintiff has conducted a reasonably diligent search and has by now produced all responsive, non-privileged documents in her possession, custody or control, she should state so in writing. For any responsive document withheld on the basis of privilege, responding party shall serve a privilege log that identifies the nature of the document, date, author, recipients, Bates numbers, and the privilege claimed. Special interrogatory No. 17: the Court reiterates its earlier tentative ruling that Plaintiff shall respond to this interrogatory. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Pltff'S Request For Production, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Continued To Jun-24-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF LAUREE PRIETO's Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions is continued to June 24, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 so that the parties can further meet and confer on the discovery that is the subject of this motion. The parties are ordered to meet and confer, and must do so in person or on the telephone, rather than by the exchange of letters or emails. By no later than June 20, 2013, the parties are to provide the Court a written update, identifying by request number those document requests in this motion, if any, that are still in dispute between them. This may be provided to the Court in the form of a letter or a short brief no more than 3 pages. There will be no additional argument in this written update. A courtesy copy of the written update must be delivered to the Court by 4:00 p.m. on June 20, 2013, clearly stating that it is to be brought to the Court's attention for the June 24, 2013 calendar. If the June 24, 2013 hearing date is not mutually convenient to the parties, the parties must meet and confer on another suitable date and be prepared to address this at the hearing on June 11, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Pltff'S Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Continued To Jun-24-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF LAUREE PRIETO's Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions is continued to June 24, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 so that the parties can further meet and confer on the discovery that is the subject of this motion. The parties are ordered to meet and confer, and must do so in person or on the telephone, rather than by the exchange of letters or emails. By no later than June 20, 2013, the parties are to provide the Court a written update, identifying by interrogatory number those interrogatories in this motion, if any, that are still in dispute between them. This may be provided to the Court in the form of a letter or a short brief of no more than 3 pages. There will be no additional argument in this written update. A courtesy copy of the written update must be delivered to the Court by 4:00 p.m. on June 20, 2013, clearly stating that it is to be brought to the Court's attention for the June 24, 2013 calendar. If the June 24, 2013 hearing date is not mutually convenient to the parties, the parties must meet and confer on another suitable date and be prepared to address this at the hearing on June 11, 2013. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Pltf And Req. For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5325.00 Against Pltf And Pltf'S Counsel
Off Calendar May-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alireza Alivandivafa, Esq. of the Law Office of Alireza Alivandivafa, Tel. 415.295.2889, for plaintiff and moving party;
Michael Burns, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw, Tel. 415.397.2823, for defendant and opposing party.

Judge Miller disclosed that she is a member of the same Inn of Court as defense counsel Ari Hersher. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. No objections were raised.

RULING: The matter was not argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT B&H EDUCATION, INC. DBA MARINELLO SCHOOLS OF BEAUTY'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5325.00 Against Plaintiff And Plaintiff's Counsel. The motion is Granted. A non-resident party witness may be deposed in California. (Glass v. Sup.Ct. (1988) 204 Cal.App.3d 1048, 1052 - 1053.) The factors enumerated in CCP 2025.260 favor compelling Plaintiff to be deposed in California. Plaintiff elected to sue in California. All of the documents, other witnesses and attorneys are in California. Defendant has offered to pay for Plaintiff's expenses and to accommodate Plaintiff's schedule. The deposition shall be limited to two consecutive days. Defendant shall pay for Plaintiff's reasonable expenses and costs to travel to California for the deposition. Requests for monetary sanctions are denied. In addition, the Court ordered the plaintiff to provide the defendant with proposed deposition dates by June 7, 2013. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Peppina Harlow, CSR # 7433 (Tel.310.557.3400)

Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss
Off Calendar May-22-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alireza Alivandivafa, Esq. of the Law Office of Alireza Alivandivafa, Tel. 415.295.2889, for plaintiff and moving party;
Michael Burns, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw, Tel. 415.397.2823, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH OR MODIFY DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS is denied. The service of the subpoena in light of Plaintiff's refusal to provide discovery information regarding URBS was neither a violation of any rule of professional conduct nor an inappropriate ex parte communication. In light of Plaintiff's discovery refusal, Defendant's actions regarding the subpoena were reasonable. Regarding the scope of the documents to be produced, Plaintiff has failed to meet her burden to demonstrate that any of the covered materials should not be produced. Among other reasons, Plaintiff has not complied with CRC 3.1345 or made a comparable showing. Plaintiff's position regarding her motion is not substantially justified. Accordingly, pursuant to CCP 1987.2(a) Defendant is awarded $5,850 in sanctions against Plaintiff and her counsel, jointly and severally. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Peppina Harlow, CSR # 7433 (Tel.310.557.3400)
